'''Tom Olbison''' (born 20 March 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[Super League]]

He has previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[2013 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Tom Olbison was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
Olbison joined [[Bradford Bulls]] from [[East Leeds A.R.L.F.C.]]<ref name=LRL110411/> and made his [[Super League]] début for the Bulls against [[Warrington Wolves]] in April 2009.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2009/apr/16/warrington-bradford-super-league |title=James Lowes backs former team-mate to haul Bulls out of their slump |newspaper=The Guardian| date=16 April 2009 |access-date=31 October 2024}}</ref>

In August 2010, Olbison scored his first try for Bradford against [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/8856501.stm |title=Bradford 22-24 Catalans Dragons |work=BBC Sport |date=1 August 2010 |access-date=31 October 2024}}</ref>

In April 2011, he signed a four-year deal to stay at Bradford until the end of the 2014 season.<ref name=LRL110411>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/olbison-rewarded-for-fine-form |title=Olbison rewarded for fine form |website=Love Rugby League |date=1 April 2011 |access-date=31 October 2024}}</ref> In July 2014, he signed a further two-year contract at Bradford who were relegated to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] at the end of the 2014 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/olbison-sidlow-commit-to-bulls |title=Olbison & Sidlow commit to Bulls |website=Love Rugby League |date=31 July 2014 |access-date=31 October 2024}}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings===

Following Bradford's liquidation, Olbison became a free agent and signed a one-year deal with [[Super League]] side [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in January 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/15004673.UPDATED__Mellor_and_Welham_latest_to_move_on_from_liquidated_Bulls|title=UPDATED: Mellor and Welham latest to move on from liquidated Bulls|work=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=5 January 2017|accessdate=31 January 2017}}</ref>
